_comment: Human-editable source for the Portrait layer, grouped by the novel's 5 chapters. Geocoded by pipeline/geocode_source.py via OpenStreetMap/Nominatim into portrait.geojson. To add a place: copy an entry, set 'group' (chapter 1-5), 'name', and a 'geocode' query (a real, findable place). Leave 'lat'/'lon' out and the geocoder fills + caches them. 'quote'/'ref' are optional and SHOULD be verified against the public-domain text (Gutenberg #4217) before publishing.

places.name: Clongowes Wood College
places.geocode: Clongowes Wood College, Clane, County Kildare, Ireland
places.gloss: Stephen's first boarding school; the square ditch, the infirmary, and the unjust pandybatting by Father Dolan.
places.quote: Once upon a time and a very good time it was there was a moocow coming down along the road and this moocow that was coming down along the road met a nicens little boy named baby tuckoo.

places.name: The Dedalus home, Bray
places.geocode: Martello Terrace, Bray, County Wicklow, Ireland
places.gloss: The bitter Christmas-dinner quarrel over Parnell between Dante, Mr Casey and Simon Dedalus.

places.name: Blackrock
places.gloss: The family's home in faded prosperity; Stephen's Monte Cristo daydreams and evening runs with Mike Flynn.

places.name: Belvedere College
places.geocode: Belvedere College, Great Denmark Street, Dublin, Ireland
places.gloss: Stephen's Jesuit day school in the city; the Whitsuntide play and his growing inward estrangement.

places.name: Cork
places.gloss: Stephen travels with his father to sell the last of the family property; he feels utterly estranged.

places.name: Queen's College, Cork
places.gloss: In his father's old anatomy theatre the word ‘Foetus’ cut into a desk jolts Stephen with shame.

places.name: Nighttown
places.gloss: The brothel quarter (Monto); at the chapter's close Stephen wanders into the arms of a prostitute.

places.name: The retreat, Belvedere College
places.geocode: Belvedere College, Great Denmark Street, Dublin, Ireland
places.gloss: The three-day retreat and the terrifying sermons on death and hell that drive Stephen to repentance.

places.name: Church Street chapel
places.geocode: St Mary of the Angels, Church Street, Dublin, Ireland
places.gloss: Across the city, in terror of damnation, Stephen makes his confession to a Capuchin priest.

places.name: Bull Wall, Dollymount
places.gloss: The wading girl — Stephen's great epiphany of beauty and his calling to art rather than priesthood.
places.quote: A girl stood before him in midstream, alone and still, gazing out to sea. She seemed like one whom magic had changed into the likeness of a strange and beautiful seabird.

places.name: North Bull Island
places.gloss: Stephen crosses the wooden bridge to the strand where, wading, he sees the bird-girl.

places.name: The director's office, Belvedere
places.geocode: Belvedere College, Great Denmark Street, Dublin, Ireland
places.gloss: The director sounds Stephen on a vocation to the priesthood — an offer he silently declines.

places.name: University College Dublin (Newman House)
places.geocode: Newman House, St Stephen's Green, Dublin, Ireland
places.gloss: Stephen the university student; the dean of studies, the aesthetic theory, the villanelle.

places.name: National Library of Ireland
places.geocode: National Library of Ireland, Kildare Street, Dublin, Ireland
places.gloss: On its steps Stephen watches the birds and argues with Cranly about belief and exile.

places.name: St Stephen's Green
places.gloss: The Green Stephen crosses daily, emblem of the city he is preparing to leave.

places.name: Kingstown Harbour (departure)
places.geocode: Dún Laoghaire Harbour, County Dublin, Ireland
places.gloss: The diary's close: Stephen resolves to leave Ireland for the continent.
places.quote: Welcome, O life! I go to encounter for the millionth time the reality of experience and to forge in the smithy of my soul the uncreated conscience of my race.
